programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: The Numbers Station
programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Lake of Death
programme.desc: Lillian and some friends travel back to the remote family cabin where her twin brother died the previous year. Soon, strange things start happening.

programme.title: Official Secrets
programme.desc: A British intelligence officer turns into a whistleblower after learning about NSA espionage plans designed to ensure passage of a resolution of war against Iraq by the U.N. Security Council in 2003.

programme.title: Rage
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping and murder of his daughter (Aubrey Peeples), a reformed criminal (Nicolas Cage) returns to his old ways to exact vengeance.

programme.title: Prisoner X
programme.desc: A CIA agent arrives at a secret underground prison to interrogate a time-traveling terrorist believed to be behind a global civil war.

programme.title: Trench 11
programme.desc: In the final days of World War I, a soldier leads a team beneath the trenches to stop a German plot that could turn the tide of the entire war.

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: White Elephant
programme.desc: When two cops witness an assassination attempt, a ruthless crime boss orders Gabriel Tancredi, an ex-Marine turned mob enforcer, to eliminate any and all threats.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Dog Eat Dog
programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Welcome to the Punch
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal is forced to return to London, it gives a veteran detective one final chance to bring the man to justice.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Official Secrets
programme.desc: A British intelligence officer turns into a whistleblower after learning about NSA espionage plans designed to ensure passage of a resolution of war against Iraq by the U.N. Security Council in 2003.

programme.title: I
programme.desc: Story of a body builder turned super model, played by Vikram, who after being deformed, exacts revenge upon those who turned him into a hunchback.

programme.title: Sahadev
programme.desc: A Journalist Uncovers The Story Of A Ruthless Assassin When She Investigates A Government Cover Up.

programme.desc: Manikarnika, The Wife Of The King Of Jhansi, Refuses To Bow Down When The East India Company Tries To Annex The Kingdom. Her Rebellion Soon Turns Into A Fiery Revolution Against The British Raj.

programme.title: Scene by Scene
programme.desc: Rod Steiger talks to Mark Cousins about his celebrated roles in On the Waterfront, Dr Zhivago and In the Heat of the Night, and shares anecdotes about James Dean, Marilyn Monroe and Elizabeth Taylor.

programme.title: Walking With...
programme.desc: Comedian and author Shappi Khorsandi walks through stunning scenery in the Peak District and along the Monsal Trail. From the hills above Tideswell, Shappi follows tracks and footpaths along Tideswell Dale and Miller’s Dale before reaching the disused railway line of the Monsal Trail. Along the way she meets residents who are charmed by her characteristic wit and ease. Taking time to appreciate the hills and ridges surrounding her, Shappi enjoys a sense of calm and composure in the country air. Walking along the trail as it tunnels through hillsides and over gorges, Shappi takes the opportunity to ‘allow the hills to hold’ her. She talks candidly about her outlook on life and love. Funny and poignant, the comedian speaks of relationships, motherhood and mental health with a refreshing honesty.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At the former Midlothian mining village of Newtongrange, Michael meets the son of a miner whose name loomed large in the disputes of the 1970s and 1980s, 'Red' Mick McGahey. On the outskirts of Edinburgh, at a recording studio in South Gyle, Michael is transported back to the wild world of 1970s pop with Bay City Rollers guitarist Stuart 'Woody' Wood. Michael leaves Edinburgh and takes the train north to Dalmeny for Queensferry, on the banks of the Firth of Forth. At the Progress Rail foundry, he marvels at the traditional skills still required to produce complex sets of rails for modern tracks in Britain and across the world. On the south bank of the Firth of Forth, Michael recalls the construction during the 1950s and 1960s of the Forth Road Bridge, which he saw as a child from the train window while crossing the rail bridge.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: Dazzling new classical arrangements of ELP, Genesis, Mike Oldfield, Yes and more, with performances from Guy Garvey, Gruff Rhys and Jane Weaver. Performing alongside the BBC Concert Orchestra and conductor Robert Ames, more is more in this ultimate rock-classical crossover. Expect synthesisers and tubular bells in a specially curated evening presented by Stuart Maconie.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: Is this the best ever cover version of Oasis' surprise football anthem of the summer?

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 February 2000 and featuring Gabrielle, Daphne & Celeste, Hepburn, Joey Negro and Taka Boom, Eiffel 65, Ian Brown, DJ Luck & MC Neat and Oasis.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 22 July 1982 and featuring The Belle Stars, Madness, Bananarama, The Brat, Trio, Junior, The Stranglers, Dollar and Irene Cara.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Peter Powell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 23 July 1986. Featuring Hollywood Beyond, Sinitta, Audrey Hall, Robert Palmer, Haywoode and Madonna.

programme.title: Prog at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the greatest names and British bands in prog rock, filmed live in the BBC studios in the early 1970s. Featuring stadium names like Yes, Genesis and Emerson, Lake and Palmer alongside much-loved bands of the era, including Caravan, Family, Atomic Rooster and more.

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Anne Nightingale introduces a recording of a Genesis concert performed at the Lyceum Ballroom, London, on 7 May 1980. The band is fronted by a bearded Phil Collins.

programme.title: The Lively Arts
programme.desc: A film, first broadcast in 1980, that portrays the many-sided talents of hugely successful British rock group Jethro Tull - at home, in the recording studio and on tour in America.

programme.title: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: Bob Harris introduces a 1977 concert by Supertramp at Queen Mary College, London. Including the hits Dreamer, Give a Little Bit and Bloody Well Right.

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Top of the Pops 2 dips into the archives to explore both classic collective hits and smash solo singles spanning 20 early years of Genesis history. From the art-rock Gabriel era of flamboyant theatrics and quirky costumes to their eighties evolution into pop superstars, Genesis are both influential experimentalists and a chart-smashing behemoth. With solo numbers from Peter Gabriel, Phil Collins and Mike + The Mechanics, this is an eclectic two-sided playlist featuring everything from I Can't Dance to I Know What I Like, No Self Control to In the Air Tonight, and Many Too Many to The Living Years. So Turn It On Again and tune in, from prog to pop, Genesis to Revelation, the hits are here on TOTP2.

programme.title: Top of the Pops

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Baghani, a Royal Bengal tiger, hunts wild boar in the Ranthambore National Park.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Some 350 million years ago, evolution reached one of its most crucial stages when fish crawled from the water onto the land and became amphibians. Today, newts, salamanders, toads and frogs still survive in great quantities, and there is even one species of frog where the male gives birth from its mouth! Presented by David Attenborough.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of three Frenchmen who couldn't stand each other, yet set off on an eight-year scientific mission in one of the most hostile places on the planet. Their plan, to settle an international row by measuring the shape of the planet, took them to the disease-ridden rainforests and oxygen-starved peaks of the Ecuadorian Andes. Rose follows in the footsteps of the 18th-century explorers who were complete innocents abroad and had no idea of the horrors they were letting themselves in for. Despite disease, death and some highly disastrous sexual liaisons, the men made discoveries that fundamentally changed all our lives.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: Irène follows a lead into a case involving Mirreau, who appears to be linked to a state secret. Meanwhile at Les Damnés, the Baron discovers that Gisèle has fled. After the tragic death of one of his brothers in arms, Gabriel wants to return to Paris to see his wife and son. In French and German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: The Sentinels must free Mazauric, the brain behind the Dyxenal research, before he divulges critical information about the French serum to the German forces. Meanwhile, the Baron receives a visit at his club from Diane, a German spy who pledges to find Gisèle in exchange for a favour. In French and German with English subtitles.

programme.title: Monster
programme.desc: When schoolboy Minato begins exhibiting strange behaviours, his mother, Sairo, suspects bullying by his teacher and confronts the school. The headmistress seems to be hiding something, and as Sairo pushes for answers, they turn out to be stranger than she imagined. In Japanese with English subtitles.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth realises that Richard is bored after taking early retirement and provides him with a hobby - being artistic with a video camera. However, it's a decision she will live to regret.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret head off for a weekend at the seaside. Victor is presented with a most unusual and unwelcome gift.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Some 350 million years ago, evolution reached one of its most crucial stages when fish crawled from the water onto the land and became amphibians. Today, newts, salamanders, toads and frogs still survive in great quantities, and there is even one species of frog where the male gives birth from its mouth! Presented by David Attenborough.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Paul Atterbury embarks on an alluring journey into the golden age of ocean liners, finding out how these great ships made such a mark on the popular imagination and why they continue to enchant to this day. Paul's voyage takes him around Britain and reveals a story of design, politics, propaganda, Hollywood glamour and tragedy. Along the way, he uncovers some amazing survivals from the liners of the past - a cinema in Scotland built from the interiors of the SS Homeric, a house in Poole in which cabins from the Mauretania are lovingly preserved - as well as the design inspiration behind the first great liners.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: The Sinfonia of London transcend in a programme brimming with colourful orchestration under the baton of their ever-popular artistic director, John Wilson. Opening with Verdi’s thunderous overture to La Forza del Destino, followed by Norwegian cellist Jonathan Aasgaard in his Proms soloist debut with Walton’s hypnotic Cello Concerto and finishing with the music time capsule that is Respighi’s enchanting sun-drenched Roman Trilogy. Presented by Katie Derham from the Royal Albert Hall.

programme.title: Not I
programme.desc: Billie Whitelaw gives a tour de force performance as a mouth suspended in space in Samuel Beckett's play.

programme.title: Performance
programme.desc: Michael Gambon stars in John Osborne's play, set in 1950s Britain, following Archie Rice, a drunken, lecherous, egotistical vaudevillian, whose act is on the downward slope.

programme.title: The Levin Interviews
programme.desc: Bernard Levin talks to playwright and screenwriter John Osborne, best known for his 1956 work Look Back in Anger.

programme.title: Searching for Sam: Adrian Dunbar on Samuel Beckett
programme.desc: Samuel Beckett has fascinated Adrian Dunbar since he was a young student. Thirty years after Beckett's death in Paris, Dunbar explores what made the man who wrote Waiting for Godot.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Trump administration has issued subpoenas to several New York Times journalists following reporting about alleged security concerns involving President Trump's new Air Force One, a plane donated by Qatar. The case has prompted debate over the balance between protecting national security and safeguarding press freedom. Claire Atkinson, founder of The Media Mix newsletter, outlines the latest developments. The BBC's latest annual report comes as the corporation warns that public service media is facing a period of jeopardy. The figures show a further fall in licence fee-paying households, alongside changing audience habits and continued financial pressures as charter renewal negotiations continue. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC's deputy director-general, sets out the corporation's position. Broadcaster and author Iain Dale reflects on a career spanning politics, publishing, blogging and radio following the publication of his memoir Have I Said Too Much?. The interview covers the enduring appeal of the radio phone-in, the influence of social media on broadcasting, and changing relationships between politicians and traditional media.

programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels to the Dominican Republic’s capital city, Santo Domingo, where baseball is a way of life. He meets one of the country’s top scouts, who gives him much-needed advice on his swing. On the outskirts of town, Clive encounters a congregation practising a hidden religious faith, while in the historic old town, he meets artisans who are reviving intricate, traditional craftwork. Then, he heads to the market with one of the country’s top chefs to pick up the ingredients for a seven-meat Dominican dish.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: A global programme that reflects the variety of takes on climate change, how best to understand it and the world's attempts to avert it, temper it or adapt to it. It is not about questioning whether climate change is happening, it's about finding the best ways to respond to it. This is sharp-edged, analytical inquiry. Hard scrutiny, touched with a sense of adventure and discovery, and where we can find it, hope. It includes stories from across the world on why we find it so hard to save our own planet, and how we might change that.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: An AI-generated actor called Tilly Norwood has been cast in the lead role of a forthcoming film, adding to the debate about artificial intelligence in filmmaking. The announcement follows criticism from actors such as Emily Blunt, and objections from the US performers' union. Eline Van der Velden, founder and CEO of Particle6, explains why she created Tilly Norwood, while Jake Kanter, international investigations editor at Deadline, sets out the reaction from the film industry. Andy Burnham has become the UK's new prime minister, and his approach to the media is already attracting attention. His team has relied more on social media, online creators and direct communication than on traditional broadcast interviews. Lizzy Buchan, political editor of the Daily Mirror; Jennifer Gerber, senior political advisor, WA Communications; Tom Nicholas, creator of the Slow News Day YouTube channel; and broadcaster Andrew Neil, presenter of The Andrew Neil Report, assess what this could mean for political journalism and public accountability.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: It's an important year for the city of Hull, in northern England. It's celebrating more than 800 years of maritime history with a raft of new museum openings that it's hoped will make it a world-class visitor attraction. Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to see the opening of the restored Spurn Lightship - literally a lighthouse on a ship - which was a mainstay on the waterways there for 50 years. She meets the people involved in the huge regeneration effort and finds out why the city's been the punchline of jokes for so many years.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: Just how long can Iran afford to keep fighting the US? War is expensive, and Iran’s economy is facing recession as people struggle with soaring prices, rising unemployment and a weak currency. But with the fighting continuing, are years of international financial sanctions really having the desired impact? Professor Djavad Salehi-Isfahani, a leading expert on Iran’s economy, tells us what he found on a recent visit to Tehran, and Mark Wallace, CEO of campaign group United Against a Nuclear Iran, explains the realities of sanctions. Plus, Ben Thompson also hears from Tom Broughton, founder of London glasses brand Cubitts, about how he’s taking on the big names in a multi-billion dollar industry.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded explores the growing backlash against artificial intelligence. As fears of job losses spread through the tech industry and protests against AI data centres gather momentum, we ask whether the promises of an AI-powered future are starting to unravel. Plus, could advanced AI one day become conscious, and if it does, what responsibilities would we have towards it? Presenter Marc Cieslak is joined by digital rights activist Cory Doctorow, AI entrepreneur Priya Lakhani and academic Dr Ella Hafermalz to unpack the biggest questions facing AI today.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: The Yellow River - running for almost five and half thousand kilometres from west to east - is known as China's mother river. It's hailed as the cradle of Chinese civilisation and over thousands of years, it has fed billions of people and inspired generations of artists. Alastair explores the Yellow River and discovers some of the finest artwork from its valley, to understand how this unique natural phenomenon is deeply connected with the identity of the Chinese and the roots of their culture.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, in the aftermath of a political earthquake in Hungary, Stephanie Hegarty explores whether the downfall of Viktor Orbán could be a warning sign for populist leaders everywhere, and BBC Eye investigates the Russian nationalists who are taking the law into their own hands.

programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, in the aftermath of a political earthquake in Hungary, Stephanie Hegarty explores whether the downfall of Viktor Orbán could be a warning sign for populist leaders everywhere, and BBC Eye investigates the Russian nationalists who are taking the law into their own hands.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: An AI-generated actor called Tilly Norwood has been cast in the lead role of a forthcoming film, adding to the debate about artificial intelligence in filmmaking. The announcement follows criticism from actors such as Emily Blunt, and objections from the US performers' union. Eline Van der Velden, founder and CEO of Particle6, explains why she created Tilly Norwood, while Jake Kanter, international investigations editor at Deadline, sets out the reaction from the film industry. Andy Burnham has become the UK's new prime minister, and his approach to the media is already attracting attention. His team has relied more on social media, online creators and direct communication than on traditional broadcast interviews. Lizzy Buchan, political editor of the Daily Mirror; Jennifer Gerber, senior political advisor, WA Communications; Tom Nicholas, creator of the Slow News Day YouTube channel; and broadcaster Andrew Neil, presenter of The Andrew Neil Report, assess what this could mean for political journalism and public accountability.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: From Manchester mayor to prime minister in a matter of months; Andy Burnham has been in politics for decades but what is his record and what does he stand for? The BBC's Joe Pike explores who is the man behind the top job.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. Young sea lion Rocky takes the biggest step of his life as he leaves the safety of his training area and joins the rest of the colony out on Half Mile Pond. As he finds his place alongside the adult sea lions and the park's newly arrived hippos, the keepers hope months of training will help him adapt to life in the open water. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben revisits the remarkable story of the park's Amur tiger cubs as Ginger and Seeka prepare to leave for new homes, while the team also celebrates 60 years since the safari park first opened its gates, transforming the way people experienced wildlife in Britain. Elsewhere, Megan meets the park's new hippos, Jon devises a new feeding challenge for the Cuban crocodiles, and the lionesses enjoy a very special birthday celebration.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: West Midlands Police are facing a massive spate of burglaries, and they know one man is responsible for them all. But they just can’t catch him in the act. However quickly police arrive, the intruder seems to have vanished into thin air. The break-ins continue unabated - one of the detectives even gets burgled himself! But a single piece of crucial information from a police informant finally gives officers the upper hand. Also, the National Crime Agency is alerted to a plumber who’s buying gun-making equipment online. Could there be an innocent explanation, or is something unusual going on in his lock-up?

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A skilled voice analyst proves the killer of a missing man is very close to home, and a UK fingerprint expert helps to overturn a miscarriage of justice.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles meets a collector with a thirst for preserving brewery history. In a converted garage in Nottinghamshire, Richard Percival has 1,300 beer trays, dating back more than 100 years. Made of heavy steel with ornate designs, they’re all striking examples of British advertising – and Eric Knowles has arranged a surprise visit for him, to recognise his record-breaking collection! In nearby Newark, two teams hope their items will serve up a profit when they go shopping with their antiques experts. Nick Hall is browsing bargains with the reds, Gary and Ian, and Izzie Balmer is with the blues, Joe and Hazel. With 60 minutes on the clock, the red team spots a pot with a rim made by a well-known silversmith and decide it’s one to think about. Meanwhile, the blues are mixing things up with a pestle and mortar. In Derbyshire, it’s almost time for last orders as auctioneer Charles Hanson gets the bids flowing.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Hairdresser Mysteries
programme.desc: When a celebrity weather reporter is found dead, Lily investigates if his zero-star reviews of all the local businesses have driven one of their own to murder.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick travels north along his favourite coastline as he recalls the catastrophic bushfires of the Black Summer of 2019 which ravaged this area of the South Coast.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple want to swap their Cheshire townhouse for the dark skies and beautiful open spaces of Shropshire. Denise Nurse helps them with the property search and also visits a flower farm in full bloom to witness at first hand how modern technology is working hand-in-hand with nature.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie and Saf clear a bungalow of quirky collectibles, including an Egyptian sarcophagus and a suffragette mannequin. However, they lose track of the time during a cuckoo clock fix.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Atlantic bluefin tuna, one of the largest fish in the sea, are making a surprise comeback off Northern Ireland’s coast, sparking an ambitious new vision for local fishing. Davy Frayne and Jonny Thursby, two anglers from the town of Ballycastle, have been fishing for giant bluefin tuna in the area for six years. Now they are attempting to establish a bluefin fishery, boasting catches of up to 1,000 pounds in weight. The stretch between Ballycastle and Rathlin is an unforgiving one, so Davy and Jonny are tested to their limits, fishing in waters where bad weather, strong currents and deep waters can create treacherous conditions. They are on the water at every opportunity, starting at the break of dawn and fishing until the light fades over the water to the west. Ballycastle is a town steeped in fishing history, where the people have longstanding connections with the waters. But in recent years, fleets have diminished and fish stocks have been low. Davy and Jonny are attempting to bring a new form of fishing to this coastline, one never seen before in these waters, and many sceptics doubt the viability of the project.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Izzie and Robin get rummaging and restoring in York. Could a tractor, a horse and a whole heap of toy cars help a special little girl get the treatment she needs?

programme.title: Granite Harbour
programme.desc: A birthday celebration ends in tragedy when a girl is found dead near a traveller site. As tensions rise, Lindo and the team uncover secrets that threaten to tear a community apart.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guest panellists Nabil Abdulrashid, Lucy Beaumont, Gethin Jones and Francesca Mills.

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue isn’t thrilled that Michael is back with Renee, his cheating wife, and turns down an invite to Renee’s BJJ graduation ceremony, pretending she has other plans and upsetting Michael in the process. Sue doesn’t have any plans, so offers to work in the charity shop to help out Eileen, but it’s a disaster. Sue realises she should go and support Michael after all and prove she’s still a good mum, but Linda causes chaos.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: Austin’s rock star persona spirals, Mel faces scrutiny over her sex life, and Ingrid discovers who’s secretly writing Big Bear.

programme.title: Austin
programme.desc: After appearing on a controversial podcast and getting Haley fired, Austin faces a family intervention. Meanwhile, Ingrid is auditioning the whole of Canberra to find an actor to play Big Bear before she loses the show. The last audition, a late entry, may be the one.

programme.title: La La Land
programme.desc: Mia (Emma Stone), an aspiring actress, and Sebastian (Ryan Gosling), a dedicated jazz musician, fall in love while struggling to make ends meet in a city known for crushing hopes and breaking hearts. Set in modern-day Los Angeles, this award-winning musical about everyday life explores the joy and pain of pursuing your dreams.

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Scott Hallsworth and Paul A Young, and by special guest Nick Knowles. Drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Boarding the train to the north coast, Anna travels along one of the most spectacular railway journeys on the island to meet Derry Girls actor Ian McElhinney. In the seaside town of Portrush, Ian reminisces about childhood holidays spent enjoying dulse, a local seaweed snack. Inspired, Anna heads to the shoreline at low tide to harvest some for a delicious recipe before moving inland, where the pair try their hand at pig farming, and produce cured meats reminiscent of the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With the last of their children about to fly the nest, a couple from a large Berkshire town need Jules Hudson’s help to find them a new home in the Suffolk countryside that has holiday let potential. Jules presents a range of fantastic options for their impressive budget, all with exciting opportunities for paying guests, leaving the couple to figure whether it will be the business or the home that takes precedence.

programme.title: Night at the Museum
programme.desc: Looking for a regular job, Larry instead gets the most irregular role of night guard at a New York museum where the exhibits come to life after sunset. Managing the menagerie and figures is tough enough, but then he has to cope with a criminal plot that aims to frame him in a robbery.

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: More famous faces step up to the challenge in a special edition of the music quiz. Competing for their chosen charities are comedy performer La Voix and quiz legend Jenny Ryan, presenter Rav Wilding and singer Chesney Hawkes, plus comedians Fatiha El-Ghorri and Harriet Kemsley. Who will identify the hits fastest and make it to the final? Get ready to shout the answers at the TV!

programme.title: Alan Carr's Picture Slam
programme.desc: Comedian Alan Carr hosts the fun and frantic quiz show where every question is a picture and every picture is worth cash. They could be anything from famous faces, places, products or logos, and teams need to name them all for a Picture Slam. Playing this time are friends Olivia and Danny from Bury St Edmunds, siblings Will and Charmain from Combe Martin, plus mum and daughter Sharon and Lucy from Staffordshire. They battle it out across four rounds, shouting out answers and building their banks. But only one team can make it to the final Super Slam for the chance to win £10,000.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: BAFTA award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre returns with a brand-new episode of his Big Show for an unmissable night of family entertainment at London's prestigious Theatre Royal Drury Lane. Hollywood star Michael Sheen hands over his phone - which is filled with A-list contacts - for an unforgettable edition of 'Send to All'. Olly Murs helps pull off an incredibly ambitious surprise on an unsuspecting superfan, and a truly deserving member of the public becomes the ‘Unexpected Star of the Show’.

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman present a special celebrity edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. The celebrities competing for the Pointless trophy are Larry Dean, Holly Walsh, Desiree Burch, Jessica Fostekew, Morgan Lake, Ashleigh Nelson, Beatie Edney and Michelle Collins.

However, Maximus proves himself a virtuoso in the arena, and his career takes him closer and closer to Rome, the Colosseum and the only thing he cares about - vengeance against Commodus.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: Nottingham Liberal Synagogue, part of the Progressive Judaism movement, guide us into the Sabbath with their musical Friday evening service, known as Erev Shabbat. Led by Rabbi Gili Zidkiyahu alongside co-Rabbi Josh Levy, this inclusive musical service welcomes all members of the community together to usher in the Sabbath. With liturgical guidance from our prayer leaders, the service blends ancient psalms and prayers, including the Sh’ma (prayer and declaration of faith) and a silent Amidah (offering time for personal reflection) with poetry and hymns. After the blessing of the challah, a traditional Jewish bread, and the Kiddush (sanctification of the wine), the community enjoy a light bring-and-share supper in the synagogue and talk about what being part of the Progressive Judaism movement means to them.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Seven celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs experience an emotional journey on a pilgrimage in north Wales, a route linked by churches dedicated to early Celtic Christian saints. Immersing themselves on this spiritual journey are wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan, who places her faith in the natural world; Spencer Matthews, a former Made in Chelsea reality star who was christened in the Church of England but is still searching for answers to life’s big questions; Sonali Shah, a journalist and TV presenter who grew up in a Jain family; comedian Eshaan Akbar, a lapsed Muslim; Amanda Lovett, a practising Catholic who was catapulted into the public eye in the first series of Traitors; actor Tom Rosenthal of Friday Night Dinner fame, who is areligious; and TV personality and former model Christine McGuinness, who is spiritual but doesn’t practise one particular faith. Travelling on foot and by bus, the seven pilgrims begin their adventure at Flint Castle on the bank of the Dee Estuary and follow the coastal path to Greenfield Valley, the official start of the 220km Pilgrim’s Way.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Buckinghamshire, Sarah Moore scours Beaconsfield recycling centre for items that can be given a new lease of life rather than hitting the skips. A retro orange armchair is a task for upholsterer Leanne Watts, while upcycling siblings Katie Stone and Sophie Lawrence want to add a 60s groove to a tallboy and dresser set. For her own project, Sarah has a vision for some discarded spectacle cases, but will they see a profit when she comes to sell?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, Eric Knowles finds out how bargain hunting can be good for the planet. Meanwhile, the reds and blues are on the hunt for profits with help from experts Charles Hanson and Nick Hall.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Laura Wright visits Ushaw Historic House in County Durham to explore the deep spiritual meaning of trees in the Bible and in Christian faith.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Eddie Marsan presents this month’s Lifeline appeal on behalf of Tourettes Action, a charity which provides support, information and advocacy to improve the lives of people living with Tourette syndrome and their families.

programme.title: The Mating Game
programme.desc: The ocean is where life first experimented with the mating game, and over time this has led to some of the most ingenious mating strategies of all.

programme.title: Dolittle
programme.desc: A doctor with a special talent for talking to animals is called upon to embark on an important quest. Joined by a young apprentice and a crew of eccentric animal friends, he must set off to find a legendary island where the healing Eden Tree grows.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Coventry mid-terrace brings back childhood memories for Dion, a former fish-and-chip shop in Cumbria has Martel feeling peckish, and there’s a bad smell in the air for Martin in the Welsh valleys.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton faces his toughest assignment to date as he attempts to find broadcasting icon and one of TV’s most opinionated panellists, Janet Street-Porter, a Suffolk sanctuary - away from the many daily irritations she’d happily banish from existence. Despite Janet’s refreshingly short and sweet wish list, it proves to be a tricky challenge satisfying this exacting house hunter. However, Alistair has an ace up his sleeve, which sees the property search take an epic detour to a remote Hebridean island.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The final stop on Anita’s east coast road trip is the mysterious Orford Ness in Suffolk. Once a top-secret military testing facility, this ten-mile shingle spit is now one of Britain's most important protected coastal landscapes, where former military buildings provide nesting sites for seabirds. Anita explores the abandoned military complex to uncover Orford Ness's secretive past and discovers how a landscape once shaped by weapons testing has been reclaimed by wildlife. She also meets an artist inspired to write an album about the area’s fascinating past and its current status as a nature haven. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the communities exposed to the sea and how nature can stage a comeback.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Sefton Park in Liverpool, where Chinese ceramics expert Lars Tharp sees a rare double Ming dynasty vase bought in a charity shop, Raj Bisram discovers some valuable Beatles memorabilia, and Dendy Easton admires a painting by George Melly. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan is almost tricked by an intriguing Grand Tour bronze, Andy McConnell falls in love with some unusual Finnish glass, and Raj is excited to see a silver disc brought in by one of the members of Gerry and the Pacemakers. Will Farmer challenges Fiona Bruce to guess the values of three vases by pioneering female artists, and Fiona discovers the history behind the famous Palm House in Sefton Park.

programme.title: The Rapture
programme.desc: Child psychologist Dr Gabrielle 'Gabs' Fox is recovering from the trauma of a car accident that killed her husband, Alex, and left her paralysed. Grieving and guilt-ridden, she has moved to the coastal town of Porth Hen in South Wales and is slowly learning to rebuild her life with the help of her own psychologist, Mel. Gabs accepts a position at Oxsmith Psychiatric Hospital, where she is assigned several new patients, including the unit’s most disturbed patient, 17-year-old Bethany Krall. Dangerous, intelligent and manipulative, Bethany was sectioned two years ago for the brutal murder of her mother, Carys Krall. The crime shocked the nation, not least because Bethany’s bereft father, Leonard Krall, is the charismatic leader of the Faith Way, a feelgood religious movement boasting millions of followers thanks to its reassuring motto 'Choose Hope'. But when Gabs reads Bethany’s case file, she finds the previous psychologist left very few notes.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: I Am Legend
programme.desc: A man who survived a deadly virus outbreak must search for a cure while avoiding the zombie-like monsters that pursue him.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Adeel Akhtar wants to know how his ancestors came from India to Kenya and makes a surprising discovery that his roots in the country run deeper than he ever imagined.

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: More and more luxury watches are being snatched off UK streets. In 2022, there was an estimated 60 per cent increase in watch thefts worldwide. It is now the crime of choice for many criminals, seen as easier and more lucrative than drug dealing. In this documentary, journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones’s £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Las Vegas has reinvented itself to pull in a new generation of visitors - recasting the city away from being the home of gambling to the world’s go-to destination for live entertainment. In this two-part series, reporter Tir Dhondy finds out how the city has changed, what this new improved version of Vegas offers today’s visitors, and how this shift is being felt by the people who live and work there.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: A helicopter trip over the vast expanse of Brazil's northern savannah helps adventurer Steve Backshall find his next contender for the Deadly 60. He is in search of the elusive giant anteater, but can he get close enough to film one for his list? A chance encounter with a tough little owl with an ingenious way of catching its supper captures Steve's attention and the rainforest lives up to its name as a very wet team ventures out at night looking for some of the world's deadliest bugs.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the Deadly crew find themselves in deep water as they head into the flooded forests of Brazil. The water is teeming with life and Steve takes a swim in a piranha-infested river in order to find out more about these killer fish. They are assured of a place on the Deadly 60 list until other mysterious fish lurking in the depths make Steve think again. Determined to see one of the strangest animals that inhabit the waters of the flooded forest, Steve and the team go in search of pink river dolphins, and an encounter with one of the most venomous snakes on the planet rounds off this flooded forest adventure.

programme.title: Horrible Histories: The Movie
programme.desc: When Roman lad Atti offends edgy Emperor Nero, he is drafted to fight in far-flung Britain, where local Celt lass Orla itches to join Boudicca’s uprising against the occupying legions. Comedy from BBC Films.

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: John and the gang go back to school to investigate the suspicious death of a headmaster. In doing so, John is faced with his own bad memories of school but is pleasantly surprised when he reconnects with his old mentor, the man who taught him everything about puzzles. With John distracted by the case, Lucy follows a new lead which she hopes will bring her closer to finding James. Lucy’s covert mission takes her to Wales, but she’s not the lone wolf she thinks she is, and her discoveries leave her rattled. A shocking revelation from IT consultant Holly forces John to question everything.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Angry, pervy, outrageous and hilarious, Fleabag arrives with a bang as she spins through the city grasping at anyone and anything that might keep her head above water.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Haunted by memories of her dead best friend, dumped by her emotionally fragile boyfriend and now desperately trying to sell her stolen goods, Fleabag attempts to rekindle romantic fires to distract her from the mayhem of her life.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag helps her inappropriate brother-in-law buy a present for Claire, who is organising her own surprise birthday party. On the hunt for a date, Fleabag reconnects with a toothy friend.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag and her sister Claire reluctantly visit a female-only silent retreat, courtesy of their father. Their enforced silence is interrupted by an unusual neighbouring weekend workshop, explosive secrets coming to light and there is a surprising connection with an old acquaintance.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: On the anniversary of her mother's death, Fleabag and Claire return to their family home for the annual memorial lunch. Although this time there is an interloper... Things get heated between Fleabag and her godmother, who muscles her way into the occasion and reveals plans for her sex-hibition.

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Fleabag takes a date to her godmother's Sex-hibition to discover a few shocks are in store for her there, including a declaration of love, a familiar piece of work and an entire wall of surprises. But where is Claire? The season finale forces Fleabag to face up to her actions and to finally reveal what happened to her beloved friend Boo.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. With his pregnant girlfriend living in a lesbian relationship, Moz shifts his attentions to his new neighbour, Judith. Meanwhile, Cartoon Head has lined up a special surprise for Derrick's stag party.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series with Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz wakes up next to someone shocking, discovers two hundred pairs of trainers in his living room, fills his kitchen with foam, and finds out that his landlord is on the way round.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series with Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Is Moz falling for his new neighbour, Judith? Is he being haunted by the ghost of Craig? And, more importantly, where can he hide the PC's crop of marijuana plants?

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series with Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz makes a startling discovery about his new love. Scary OAP Mrs Coneybear arrives to perform a seance, and a plain clothes cop becomes trapped in the loft.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series with Johnny Vegas as a small time dope dealer Moz. Cartoon Head and Psycho Paul try to get Moz involved in their kidnapping plan. Moz point blank refuses, until he receives a visit from terrifying gang boss Stemroach.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Life is hard, so Callum and Jordan, two emotionally unassembled young lads from Northern Ireland, start the Funboys’ Fun Club: a playgroup for childish young men to engage in wholesome mucking about. But when a juice party goes awry, Callum’s ultra-religious fiancée Morgan kicks them out, and the Funboys must search for a new HQ. When they come across Marty, a catatonically depressed 60-year-old, can they bring joy back to his life and prove that fun won't necessarily lead to an eternity spent in raging hellfire?

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: This terrifying story is assembled from footage discovered in the wreckage of Le Verrier Space Station.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara, with their old friend Rigsy, find themselves in a magical alien world, hidden on a street in the heart of London. Sheltered within are some of the most fearsome creatures of the universe, and Ashildr! With a death sentence hanging over their heads, not all of the intruders will get out alive.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee and Lucy are asked to cover for Anna on more than one occasion, so that she can go out at night and have secret Italian lessons, they suspect the worst – that she is having an affair. Lee decides that it is up to him to tell Toby that his marriage is in jeopardy and sets about finding the evidence.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee's lifestyle catches up on him and he is forced to wait on a hospital ward for routine surgery on his gallbladder until his blood pressure comes down. But visits from the parents-in-law and Anna and Toby, who arrive to ask him a question he doesn't want to hear, ensure that his stress levels remain dangerously high.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: When Agnes's family starts making comments about her weight, she decides to go on a diet and quickly discovers just how difficult it is to overcome her craving for fish and chips. Cathy brings her new, older man home. He is a psychiatrist and seems much more interested in Agnes than in Cathy. Meanwhile, Rory has a professional dilemma when he discovers some illegal drugs at work.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Spartacus, the old family dog, is looking poorly. Reluctantly, the family decide the time has come to put her in a home. However, when the Brown children gather together to decide how to break the sad news to their mother, Agnes gets the wrong end of the stick. Under the impression that her children are planning on putting her in a nursing home, a furious Agnes sets out to persuade her children she is not going senile.

programme.title: Ghosts Australia
programme.desc: The Australian version of the hit UK series. When city couple Kate and Sean inherit Ramshead Manor, a run-down country estate, they see it as a sign for a fresh new start. But after Kate has a near-death experience, she gains the ability to see and hear ghosts – and discovers there are several living in the mansion. Kate must navigate the ghosts' issues while trying to start a hotel business with Sean, who remains unable to see them.

programme.title: Ghosts Australia
programme.desc: The Australian version of the hit UK series. When city couple Kate and Sean inherit Ramshead Manor, a run-down country estate, they see it as a sign for a fresh new start. But after Kate has a near-death experience, she gains the ability to see and hear ghosts – and discovers there are several living in the mansion. Kate must navigate the ghosts' issues while trying to start a hotel business with Sean, who remains unable to see them.

programme.title: Ghosts Australia
programme.desc: The Australian version of the hit UK series. When city couple Kate and Sean inherit Ramshead Manor, a run-down country estate, they see it as a sign for a fresh new start. But after Kate has a near-death experience, she gains the ability to see and hear ghosts – and discovers there are several living in the mansion. Kate must navigate the ghosts' issues while trying to start a hotel business with Sean, who remains unable to see them.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: At an estate sale, Johnny and Moira find a mattress in its original wrapping and then promptly get into a bidding war over it. Mutt surprises Alexis with a new bike but soon discovers why it might be an inappropriate gift. David reluctantly agrees to help Roland shop for clothing.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: In an attempt to show off his acumen, Johnny pitches a business idea that Bob takes far too seriously. David asks Stevie to take him to a job interview in Elmdale, only to discover that he has previously offended the store’s owner. Moira overcomes her squeamishness in order to take care of a sick Alexis.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Fed up with her town looking like a rubbish heap, Moira tries to effect change by voicing her complaints to the town council. Alexis’s relationship is thrown into jeopardy when Mutt makes the rash decision to update his look. David takes full advantage of his company credit card, and it’s up to Johnny to reel him in before it’s too late.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: The team gets a visit from a government minister, to whom Mary unexpectedly takes a shine. Nick tries to get rid of him with a questionable plan involving high explosives.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Bird hatches a plan to stop Nick and Mary going on about each other once and for all. The lieutenant colonel persuades Towerblock to pimp his ride.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick's hunt for the Taliban bomber hots up. Simon's lack of trust in Mac and Rocket comes back to bite him. Bird finds herself the reluctant participant in a girlie chat with Mary.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: This terrifying story is assembled from footage discovered in the wreckage of Le Verrier Space Station.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)

Sheltered within are some of the most fearsome creatures of the universe, and Ashildr! With a death sentence hanging over their heads, not all of the intruders will get out alive.
